title: Dried-Tagine Italian Pescatarian Tagine
description: A comforting Italian pescatarian dish featuring gochugaru and tadka in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of pescatorial oil in a large ceramic tagine over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the gochugaru and sauté for 1 minute until the aroma is released.
Add the mussels and shrimp and sauté for 2-3 minutes until they start to turn opaque.
Add the fregula and clam juice, reserved 1/4 cup for tadka, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10-15 minutes until the fregula is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Transfer the tagine to the preheated oven and cook for 5-7 minutes until the flavors have melded together and the fregula is cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat the reserved 1/4 cup of clam juice in a small skillet over medium-high heat until it starts to simmer and slightly reduce, known as tadka; set aside.
Stir the chopped parsley into the tagine and serve hot, garnished with the tadka on top.
Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
